content:
# Final Review Proposal
**Producer:** agent:codex/gpt-5.4

## Summary

Found 2 correctness gaps in the degradation work: the new rate-limit matcher is too aggressive for some transient 429s, and `process_backend` still misses stdout-only exhaustion errors.

## Amendments

1. Tighten the new rate-limit exhaustion heuristic before shipping it. `is_backend_exhausted` currently upgrades any `rate limit` message with `retry after 60 sec` (and other broad `until <digit>` forms) to `BackendExhausted` in `src/adapters/process_backend.rs:1865-1903`, and `openrouter_backend::map_http_error` consumes that helper before falling back to retryable 429 handling in `src/adapters/openrouter_backend.rs:349-370`. That means a normal transient 429 with a one-minute reset window will now bypass retries and permanently drop the backend, which violates the requirement to preserve retry behavior for genuinely transient errors.
   Rationale: This changes recoverable rate limits into terminal failures, so healthy backends can be removed from a run unnecessarily.
2. Add a path for stdout-only exhaustion messages on process-backed CLIs. The non-zero-exit classifier explicitly ignores raw stdout and only inspects stderr plus structured `is_error` envelopes (`src/adapters/process_backend.rs:1448-1456`, `src/adapters/process_backend.rs:1490-1498`), and the helper comment admits plain-text stdout-only quota messages are missed (`src/adapters/process_backend.rs:1827-1830`). If a backend prints `usage limit` / `quota exceeded` only to stdout, it will still be classified as `TransportFailure` and burned through the retry loop instead of degrading gracefully.
   Rationale: This leaves one of the required output channels uncovered, so the feature still fails for a realistic backend-emission mode.
